title: Intrusion Detection / IDS Alert
symptoms: IDS/IPS signature triggered for known exploit or scan pattern
Unusual traffic spike from a single source IP
Multiple failed connection attempts to internal systems
SIEM correlation rule fired for lateral movement or data exfiltration pattern
steps: Identify the source IP, destination IP, and triggered signature from the IDS alert
Check if the source IP is internal or external — internal sources indicate possible compromise
Verify the alert is not a false positive: check the payload/context in SIEM or IDS console
Check firewall logs for the same source/destination pair in the last 24 hours
Determine if the attack was blocked (IPS mode) or only detected (IDS mode)
If internal source: isolate the host immediately from the network — disable port on switch
If external source: verify firewall ACL is blocking the source IP; add block if not
Capture packet sample for forensic analysis if the threat is ongoing
resolution: Block the offending source IP at the perimeter firewall. If internal host is compromised: isolate (VLAN quarantine or port shutdown), notify Security team for forensic analysis. Update IDS signature database. File security incident report in GLPI with full timeline. Notify CISO if data exfiltration is suspected.
escalation: Any confirmed intrusion (not just alert) is a P1 Security Incident — page Security Engineer on-call immediately regardless of time. If sensitive data systems are targeted, notify CISO and Legal within 1 hour.
estimated_resolution_time: Initial containment: 15-30 minutes. Full investigation: 2-48 hours.
reference: Emircom Security Incident Response Plan — CSIRT-SOP-003
